corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: In this section, we show that DDIMs outperform DDPMs in terms of image generation when fewer iterations are considered, giving speed ups of 10× to 100× over the original DDPM generation process.

The non-Markovian forward process presented here seems to suggest continuous forward processes other than Gaussian (which cannot be done in the original diffusion framework, since Gaussian is the only stable distribution with finite variance).

For each dataset, we use the same trained model with T = 1000 and the objective being L γ from Eq. ( ) with γ = 1; as we argued in Section 3, no changes are needed with regards to the training procedure.
We use the same model for each dataset, and only compare the performance of different generative processes.
Our CelebA model has five feature map resolutions from 64 × 64 to 4 × 4, and we use the original CelebA dataset (not CelebA-HQ) using the from the StyleGAN repository.
To accelerate sampling, we present denoising diffusion implicit models (DDIMs), a more efficient class of iterative implicit probabilistic models with the same training procedure as DDPMs.
To close this efficiency gap between DDPMs and GANs, we present denoising diffusion implicit models (DDIMs).
In Section 3, we generalize the forward diffusion process used by DDPMs, which is Markovian, to non-Markovian ones, for which we are still able to design suitable reverse generative Markov chains.
DDIMs can also be used to encode samples that reconstruct them from the latent code, which DDPMs cannot do due to the stochastic sampling process.
Our architecture for θ (x t ) follows that in , which is a U-Net based on a Wide ResNet .

The non-Markovian forward process presented here seems to suggest continuous forward processes other than Gaussian (which cannot be done in the original diffusion framework, since Gaussian is the only stable distribution with finite variance).
